basics.name: Jess Jones
basics.label: PhD candidate, Robotics & Autonomous Systems
basics.summary: PhD candidate at the University of Bristol with a First Class BSc in Computer Science. Interested in deep learning, multi-agent reinforcement learning, and vision-based computing for robotics and trustworthy analysis of online content. Background combines research engineering with leadership in hospitality and an indoor farming start-up.

work.name: CASM Technology
work.summary: Tools using foundation models and machine learning for analysis of social media, misinformation, and harmful content.
highlights: Research and development on NLP and ML systems for real-world audit and safety workflows.

work.name: University of Bristol — High Performance Computing Group
work.summary: Eight-week summer internship benchmarking MGCFD-OP2 on Arm architectures.
highlights: Released mgcfd-op2 and babelstream packages to the Spack repository.

work.name: Motion Impossible
work.summary: Architecture and UI for multi-robot camera dolly control.
highlights: Designed system for simultaneous control and interaction of multiple robotic camera dollies.
Implemented networking for one-to-one, one-to-many, and many-to-many device interactions.

work.name: CASM Technology
work.summary: Summer placements supporting PhD-level NLP research and fact-checking systems.
highlights: Custom entity linking in an automated fact-checking pipeline.
Research contributing to a UK Cabinet Office report on technologies for operational delivery.

work.name: Herb & Bloom
work.summary: Indoor vertical farming start-up operating as a PLC.
highlights: Established and ran day-to-day operations and management of the business.

work.name: Cubitt House
work.summary: Premium pub and hotel group (~£14m turnover).
highlights: Operational delivery across sites, performance, procurement, and strategic planning.
Hargrave Prize for best final-year BSc thesis.
NAG HPC Student Prize (second highest accreditation, HPC programme).

awards.title: Hargrave Prize
awards.summary: Best final-year undergraduate thesis on the BSc Computer Science programme.

awards.title: NAG HPC Student Prize
awards.summary: Second highest accreditation on the High Performance Computing programme (2023).

awards.title: Netcraft Prize
awards.summary: Awarded to the top ten second-year Computer Science students (2022).

publications.name: Assessing VLM-Driven Semantic Affordance Inference for Non-Humanoid Robot Morphologies
publications.summary: Evaluates vision-language models for zero-shot affordance inference on non-humanoid robots, including conservative bias with implications for safe deployment.

references.name: Prof. Sabine Hauert
references.reference: PhD supervisor, University of Bristol — sabine.hauert@bristol.ac.uk

projects.name: MARL framework for abstract communication (DOTS swarms)
projects.summary: FARSCOPE taught component — multi-agent reinforcement learning for Bristol swarm robotics.
highlights: Simulation framework for efficient MARL experiments on DOTS robots.
Novel paint-mixing scenario testing communication and interpretation.
Compared MARL algorithms and network architectures.

projects.name: 3D corallite reconstruction (BSc thesis)
projects.summary: Volumetric ViT and topological loss for segmenting coral micro-CT data (Hargrave Prize).
highlights: UNet–ViT pipeline on volumetric inputs; Blender API for interactive 3D analysis.

projects.name: Lattice–Boltzmann HPC (NAG prize project)
projects.summary: Distributed CFD mini-apps with OpenMP, MPI, and OpenCL (NAG HPC Student Prize).
highlights: Architecture-specific optimisations, multi-node scaling, GPU offloading.
